title: ～とすれば
titleEn: Used to express 'if it were the case that...', 'if we assume that...'.
structure: Verb-casual + とすれば / い-Adjective + とすれば / な-Adjective + だとすれば / Noun + だとすれば
structureEn: Verb-casual + とすれば / い-Adjective + とすれば / な-Adjective + だとすれば / Noun + だとすれば
explanation: Used to express 'if it were the case that...', 'if we assume that...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とすれば grammar point poses a hypothetical condition and then draws a conclusion or inference. It translates as 'if we suppose/assume that...' or 'if it is the case that...'.

title: ～となったら
titleEn: Used to express 'if it comes to...', 'when it becomes the case that...'.
structure: Verb-casual + となったら / Noun + となったら
structureEn: Verb-casual + となったら / Noun + となったら
explanation: Used to express 'if it comes to...', 'when it becomes the case that...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～となったら grammar point is used to describe a situation where, if matters progress to that stage, a certain action or outcome will follow. It’s often translated as 'when it comes to...', 'if it comes to...'.

title: ～となると
titleEn: Used to highlight a certain circumstance; 'when it comes to...', 'if it becomes...', 'in the event that...'.
structure: Verb-casual + となると / い-Adjective + となると / な-Adjective + だとなると / Noun + だとなると
structureEn: Verb-casual + となると / い-Adjective + となると / な-Adjective + だとなると / Noun + だとなると
explanation: Used to highlight a certain circumstance; 'when it comes to...', 'if it becomes...', 'in the event that...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～となると grammar point highlights a specific situation or condition and indicates what normally or naturally happens under that circumstance. It can be translated as 'when it comes to...', 'if it becomes...', or 'in the event that...'.

title: ～となれば
titleEn: Expresses the idea of 'if it comes to...; when we talk about...; in the case of...'.
structure: Verb-casual + となれば, い-Adjective + となれば, な-Adjective + だとなれば, Noun + だとなれば
structureEn: Verb-casual + となれば, い-Adjective + となれば, な-Adjective + だとなれば, Noun + だとなれば
explanation: Expresses the idea of 'if it comes to...; when we talk about...; in the case of...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～となれば grammar point is used to express a situation where a particular case or condition brings about a certain result or consequence. It translates as 'if it comes to...; when we talk about...; in the case of...' in English.

title: ～とのことだ
titleEn: Expresses something that the speaker heard; 'I've heard that', 'I was told that'.
explanation: Expresses something that the speaker heard; 'I've heard that', 'I was told that'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とのことだ grammar point is used to express something that the speaker has heard from someone else. It can be roughly translated as 'I've heard that', 'I was told that' in English. It is a formal way to present hearsay or information the speaker did not directly witness but was informed about.

title: ～とはいえ
structure: Noun + とはいえ, な-Adjective + だとはいえ, い-Adjective + とはいえ, Verb-casual + とはいえ
structureEn: Noun + とはいえ, な-Adjective + だとはいえ, い-Adjective + とはいえ, Verb-casual + とはいえ
explanation: Even though; although; but (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とはいえ grammar structure is used to express the contradictory nature of a situation or event. It means 'even though' or 'although', signifying a sense of contrast or contradiction. The usage of this structure is mainly seen in a formal or written context.

title: ～とみえて
structure: Verb-casual + とみえて, い-Adjective + とみえて, な-Adjective + だとみえて, Noun + だとみえて
structureEn: Verb-casual + とみえて, い-Adjective + とみえて, な-Adjective + だとみえて, Noun + だとみえて
explanation: It seems that; apparently (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とみえて grammar point is used to express the speaker's judgment about a situation based on visible or known information. It is often translated as 'apparently' or 'it seems that' in English.

title: ～とみられる
titleEn: It is considered that; It is thought that; It is believed that
structure: Verb-casual + とみられる, Noun + とみられる
structureEn: Verb-casual + とみられる, Noun + とみられる
explanation: It is considered that; It is thought that; It is believed that (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とみられる grammar point is used to express how an idea, fact, or circumstance is considered or understood. This phrase is often used in news or reports to show the speaker's or writer's estimation, judgment, or present belief about something.

title: ～とみると
titleEn: Expresses the concept of 'when one sees it as', 'viewed as', or 'considering'.
structure: Verb-casual + とみると, い-Adjective + とみると, な-Adjective + だとみると, Noun + だとみると
structureEn: Verb-casual + とみると, い-Adjective + とみると, な-Adjective + だとみると, Noun + だとみると
explanation: Expresses the concept of 'when one sees it as', 'viewed as', or 'considering'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The ～とみると grammar point is used to express the concept of seeing something from a specific perspective and responding accordingly. It often brings awareness toward something or a change in perception. It is translated as 'when one sees it as', 'viewed as', or 'considering'.

title: どんなに～うが
structure: どんなに + Verb-volitional + うが, どんなに + い-Adjective(～かろう) + が, どんなに + な-Adjective + だろうが
structureEn: どんなに + Verb-volitional + うが, どんなに + い-Adjective(～かろう) + が, どんなに + な-Adjective + だろうが
explanation: Expresses 'no matter how much...', 'even if...'. (Ngu?n: Hanabira; b?n d?ch Vi?t c?n bi?n t?p.)
explanationEn: The grammar point どんなに～うが is used to express a condition that is not affected by the degree of an action or state. It can be translated as 'no matter how much...' or 'even if...'. It is generally used with verbs, い-adjectives and な-adjectives in their volitional or かろう/だろう form.
